name: My boss in Ogden says my work injury isn't covered because it happened on break, is that true?
description: No coverage for breaks/commutes in Utah; must be during job duties.[1]

mainEntity.name: My boss in Ogden says my work injury isn't covered because it happened on break, is that true?

acceptedAnswer.text: No coverage for breaks/commutes in Utah; must be during job duties.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Injuries on break or commute are typically not covered under Utah workers' comp, as benefits apply only to duties performed on the job or work errands.[1] Coverage includes traumatic injuries like slips or occupational illnesses.[1] Key factors: Prove injury occurred during required duties; off-duty incidents excluded. Report anyway within 180 days for evaluation.[1][2] Practical guidance: Gather witness statements or logs showing work context; see doctor and note work relation. If denied, appeal via Utah Labor Commission. No-fault system prioritizes quick reporting over fault debates.[2]
